news.title: South Korea in post-study visa push amid shift towards quality
news.summary_en: After surpassing its “Study Korea 300K” target ahead of schedule, South Korea says it now wants to shift focus towards “strategic quality management”, post-graduation opportunities and long-term talent retention.
news.author: Abhishek Nair

news.title: Jacqui Smith: UK universities must prove local value
news.summary_en: The UK minister for skills, Baroness Jacqui Smith, has reiterated the government’s support for international education, while urging universities to do more to combat public scepticism of higher education.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: The PIEoneer Awards 2026 shortlist revealed
news.summary_en: The shortlist for The PIEoneer Awards 2026 has been revealed, marking a major milestone as the international education sector’s flagship event celebrates its 10th anniversary this year.
news.author: The PIE News team

news.title: US: Duration of status elimination moves to final review
news.summary_en: Replacing 'duration of status' with fixed time limits on student and visitor visas has moved one step closer to becoming a reality in the US, as the final rule is submitted for White House approval.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: Who’s in the room? Inside the new Education Sector Action Group
news.summary_en: Advisory and core members now make up the government’s new Education Sector Action Group (ESAG), the minister-led body tasked with driving the UK’s international education strategy.
news.author: Kim Martin

news.title: Wales’ Taith program expands access to international learning
news.summary_en: Wales' international learning exchange program is widening access to global mobility opportunities by opening up access to those who have not traditionally been internationally mobile.
news.author: Beth Kennedy

news.title: “Small violin” for UK university funding woes as sector urged to rebuild trust
news.summary_en: A new report from the UCL Policy Lab argues that UK universities must take the lead in rebuilding public trust and renewing their civic purpose if they are to thrive in the years ahead.
news.author: Kim Martin
